victim_background: Dr. Emily Carter was a leading researcher in biochemical engineering at the university. Her groundbreaking discoveries had the potential to disrupt major pharmaceutical companies, leading to significant financial loss. Several colleagues envied her success, and some felt threatened by her influence in grant proposals.
method: poisoning her coffee with a rare neurotoxin that he synthesized for research purposes
motive: Dr. Michael Reynolds was deeply in love with Emily but felt rejected and humiliated after she chose to partner with another colleague on a major grant proposal instead of him. His obsession turned to rage, leading him to eliminate her.

suspects.name: Dr. Sarah Mitchell
suspects.means: Access to all lab materials and equipment needed for experiments, including poisons used in research.
suspects.motive: Sarah had previously collaborated with Emily but was overlooked for a promotion that Emily received, creating a motive rooted in jealousy.
suspects.opportunity: Sarah often worked late nights in the lab and was known to be present during crucial hours.
suspects.alibi: She claims she was in the library studying during the murder.
suspects.alibi_real_reason: She was in the lab, trying to modify Emily's recent experiment for her own research.

suspects.name: Dr. Mark Thompson
suspects.means: Frequent access to all lab areas and knowledge of chemical combinations.
suspects.motive: Mark had a secret crush on Emily and felt heartbroken after she rejected his advances.
suspects.opportunity: He had been working in the lab at the time of the murder and could easily access the coffee station.
suspects.alibi: He claims to have been taking inventory in the main office during the murder.

suspects.name: Dr. Jennifer Lee
suspects.means: Has access to the lab's coffee supplies and works with the same equipment as Emily.
suspects.motive: She recently had a public argument with Emily regarding research integrity, which caused her to harbor resentment.
suspects.opportunity: Jennifer was in the lab during the murder, working on a presentation.
suspects.alibi: Claims to have been in the break room during the time of the murder.
suspects.alibi_real_reason: She was actually in the lab, overhearing the argument between Emily and Michael.

suspects.name: Dr. Michael Reynolds
suspects.means: He has the highest level of access to the lab and control over the equipment.
suspects.motive: He killed Emily out of personal betrayal after she rejected him for a colleague, leading to feelings of inadequacy and rage.
suspects.opportunity: Michael was in a meeting across campus but arrived at the lab shortly after the murder took place.
suspects.alibi: He states he was in a meeting, confirmed by several colleagues.
suspects.alibi_real_reason: He left the meeting early, claiming he needed to address a 'lab emergency.'

evidence_chain.description: A coffee mug with Emily's name on it found in the lab's coffee station
evidence_chain.points_to: Indicates Emily was in the lab at the time of her murder.
evidence_chain.discovery_method: Witnesses saw Emily in the lab before her death.

evidence_chain.description: A security camera footage showing Michael leaving the lab shortly after Emily was last seen
evidence_chain.points_to: Suggests that Michael was present at the scene.

evidence_chain.description: A toxicology report confirming the presence of a rare neurotoxin in Emily's bloodstream
evidence_chain.discovery_method: Detective receives lab results during the investigation.

evidence_chain.description: A witness statement from Jennifer stating she overheard an argument between Michael and Emily
evidence_chain.points_to: Points to a motive for Michael to want to harm Emily.

evidence_chain.description: Lab records showing Michael had access to the neurotoxin used for the poisoning
evidence_chain.points_to: Indicates Michael had means to commit the murder.
evidence_chain.discovery_method: Detective requests access logs from lab.

evidence_chain.description: A late-night access log showing Michael entered the lab at a time he claimed to be in a meeting
evidence_chain.discovery_method: Detective checks access logs after uncovering Michael's motive.

red_herrings.description: A coffee cup with Sarah's fingerprints was found near Emily's desk.
red_herrings.planted_evidence: The forensic report indicates that the coffee cup had traces of the rare neurotoxin.
red_herrings.debunk_method: The detective finds security footage showing Sarah leaving her coffee cup in the break room well before the murder occurred, disproving any connection.

red_herrings.description: A series of emails between Mark and Emily discussing personal feelings that escalated before the murder.
red_herrings.planted_evidence: Screenshots of the emails hinting at an argument over Emily's 'dismissive attitude' towards Mark's contributions.
red_herrings.debunk_method: The detective uncovers the full email thread indicating Mark was actually offering to help Emily with her project, revealing no animosity and proving the emails were taken out of context.

red_herrings.description: A lab report with Jennifer's notes was found crumpled in Emily's wastebasket with annotations about Emily's experiments.
red_herrings.planted_evidence: The notes appear to indicate disagreements on methodologies that could have led to a confrontation.
red_herrings.debunk_method: Forensic analysis shows that the crumpled notes were dated weeks before the murder and had nothing to do with the current project, demonstrating no immediate motive.
